articles.title: India 5G enterprise rollout accelerates: 50,000 private 5G networks expected by FY2028
articles.summary: India's 5G enterprise deployment is accelerating with DoT announcing a simplified private 5G network licensing framework. Manufacturing, mining, ports, and logistics sectors are the primary adopters, with over 50,000 enterprise private networks expected by FY2028, each creating demand for IoT sensors, edge computing, and industrial automation systems.

articles.title: Reliance Jio launches 5G IoT platform for Indian manufacturers: 200 factories onboarded
articles.summary: Reliance Jio has launched its JioThings 5G IoT platform targeting Indian manufacturers, with 200 factories already connected. The platform provides real-time machine telemetry, predictive maintenance alerts, and quality control integration using ultra-low-latency 5G connectivity.

articles.title: Smart factory adoption in India: 35% of large manufacturers deploying Industry 4.0 systems
articles.summary: A NASSCOM-Deloitte survey reveals that 35% of large Indian manufacturers have deployed or are actively deploying Industry 4.0 systems including IoT sensors, robotics, and AI-powered quality inspection, driven by export competitiveness pressure and rising labor costs.

articles.title: Last-mile delivery optimization: Delhivery and Ecom Express deploy AI routing on 5G
articles.summary: Delhivery and Ecom Express have both deployed AI-powered last-mile delivery optimization using 5G-connected route planning and real-time traffic integration, reporting 22% improvement in delivery efficiency and 18% reduction in fuel costs per delivery.

articles.title: Cold chain IoT sensors market in India to reach ₹8,000 crore by 2028
articles.summary: India's cold chain IoT sensor market is projected to grow at 38% CAGR to reach ₹8,000 crore by 2028, driven by pharma export compliance requirements, food safety regulations, and the expansion of organized cold storage infrastructure for perishable exports.

articles.title: Predictive maintenance platforms: Indian steel and cement manufacturers report 40% downtime reduction
articles.summary: JSW Steel, Dalmia Bharat, and Ultratech Cement have reported 40% reduction in unplanned equipment downtime after deploying IoT-based predictive maintenance platforms, with AI models detecting bearing failures and motor degradation 3-4 weeks before failure.

articles.title: India's manufacturing sector IoT investment crosses ₹25,000 crore in FY2026
articles.summary: Indian manufacturing companies collectively invested ₹25,000 crore in IoT infrastructure in FY2026, a 52% increase year-on-year, driven by PLI scheme performance incentives requiring digital traceability of production and quality metrics.

articles.title: 5G enabled smart logistics: Blue Dart and DTDC deploy real-time shipment tracking at 500+ hubs
articles.summary: Blue Dart and DTDC have deployed 5G-enabled IoT tracking across 500+ logistics hubs, enabling real-time shipment location, temperature monitoring for sensitive cargo, and automated exception management — reducing customer escalations by 34%.

articles.title: DoT spectrum policy: enterprise 5G private networks get dedicated mmWave allocation for factories
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications has allocated dedicated mmWave spectrum for enterprise private 5G networks in manufacturing zones, significantly reducing latency for robotic automation and enabling ultra-reliable low-latency communications required for safety-critical industrial applications.

articles.title: Mahindra Logistics launches AI-powered warehouse management for automotive supply chain
articles.summary: Mahindra Logistics has deployed an AI-powered warehouse management system across 45 automotive industry warehouses, integrating with 5G-connected IoT inventory sensors to achieve 99.7% inventory accuracy and 28% improvement in warehouse throughput.

articles.title: India pharma cold chain: FSSAI mandates IoT temperature logging for export shipments by June 2026
articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India has mandated real-time IoT temperature logging for all pharmaceutical export shipments by June 2026, requiring GPS-tracked, tamper-evident cold chain containers with 30-day data retention.

source_report: India Digital Infrastructure Report 2025: 5G rollout accelerating enterprise IoT adoption across manufacturing and logistics
